# 002_InlineAssembly

Inline assembly examples where instructions like MOV, ADD, LDR, MRS and STR are used to demonstrate how processor manages
the Core registers to execute written programs

## Assembly code generated by the compiler
- small explanations are given for each inline assembly instruction
```
08000204 <main>:
  __asm volatile (code : output operand list : input operand list : clobber list);

- code part is always written in double quotes
*/
int main(void)
{
 8000204:	b480      	push	{r7}
 8000206:	b087      	sub	sp, #28
 8000208:	af00      	add	r7, sp, #0

	//store val of r0 into r2
	__asm volatile("STR R0,[R2]");
#endif

	int val=50;
 800020a:	2332      	movs	r3, #50	@ 0x32
 800020c:	617b      	str	r3, [r7, #20]
	//%0 signifies the 1st operand in this case val
	//more input operands can be added by giving a comma after val
	//r tells the compiler to use registers for data manipulation
	__asm volatile("MOV R0,%0": :"r"(val));
 800020e:	697b      	ldr	r3, [r7, #20]
 8000210:	4618      	mov	r0, r3

	//using immediate value skips the register being used
	__asm volatile("MOV R0,%0": :"i"(0x51));
 8000212:	f04f 0051 	mov.w	r0, #81	@ 0x51

	//in this case the value 0x50 is moved into a reg and then the reg is used to shift
	//value to r0
	__asm volatile("MOV R0,%0": :"r"(0x53));
 8000216:	2353      	movs	r3, #83	@ 0x53
 8000218:	4618      	mov	r0, r3

	uint32_t control_reg;
	//=r modifier suggests the compiler that this a write only operand
	//MRS is Move from Special Register to general register
	//MSR is Move from general register to Special Register
	asm volatile("MRS %0,CONTROL":"=r"(control_reg));
 800021a:	f3ef 8314 	mrs	r3, CONTROL
 800021e:	613b      	str	r3, [r7, #16]

	int var1=10, var2;
 8000220:	230a      	movs	r3, #10
 8000222:	60fb      	str	r3, [r7, #12]
	//move value in var1 to var2
	asm volatile("MOV %0,%1":"=r"(var2):"r"(var1));
 8000224:	68fb      	ldr	r3, [r7, #12]
 8000226:	461b      	mov	r3, r3
 8000228:	60bb      	str	r3, [r7, #8]

	int p1, *p2;
	p2=(int*)0x20000008;
 800022a:	4b03      	ldr	r3, [pc, #12]	@ (8000238 <main+0x34>)
 800022c:	607b      	str	r3, [r7, #4]
	//load val of p2 (pointer) into p1
	//for ldr instruction the source is specified like this [source]
	asm volatile("LDR %0,[%1]":"=r"(p1):"r"(p2));
 800022e:	687b      	ldr	r3, [r7, #4]
 8000230:	681b      	ldr	r3, [r3, #0]
 8000232:	603b      	str	r3, [r7, #0]
```
